summary: Verified ADR 0169 live on the production platform-context API path after shipping the missing runtime helper bundle, fixing corpus-root-relative observability defaults, and manually replaying the build-server-to-runtime apply path when the ansible jump transport stayed unstable.

verification.observed: Focused regression coverage passed with `11 passed in 0.89s` for `tests/test_platform_context_service.py` and `tests/test_rag_context_runtime_role.py`, followed by `./scripts/validate_repo.sh ansible-syntax`.
On `docker-runtime-lv3`, `/v1/context/query` answered the step-ca question with cited matches including `docs/adr/0042-step-ca-for-ssh-and-internal-tls.md`.

verification.check: Host proxy health and authenticated query
verification.observed: From the controller, `http://100.64.0.1:8010/healthz` returned `{"status":"ok","collection":"platform_context"}` and the proxied authenticated query returned cited matches for the step-ca question.

notes: Repeated ansible live-apply attempts from the build-server runner hit intermittent SSH banner-exchange timeouts on the `docker-runtime-lv3` jump path even when direct SSH from the same build server succeeded.

The final live apply used the tested repository artifacts from commit `550fd37645f66e25d0d195123efc17b6a9458a04`, copied them from the build-server session workspace onto `docker-runtime-lv3`, rebuilt the `platform-context-api` image, restarted Docker once to restore the missing nat chain, and recreated the full compose stack to restore service-to-service DNS for Qdrant.

The host-side Tailscale proxy for `platform-context-api` was already present as `lv3-tailscale-proxy-platform-context-api.socket`; the earlier proxy failure was a startup race while the guest service was still converging.
